title: limnanthes gracilis subsp. parishii
intro: Limnanthes gracilis subsp. parishii, commonly known as Parish's meadowfoam, is a flowering plant species belonging to the meadowfoam family (Limnanthaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Parish's meadowfoam include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin conditions, and exhibit antioxidant properties. It is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, salves, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
